title: Towards a Neural Debugger for Python
authors: Maximilian Beck, Jonas Gehring, Jannik Kossen, Gabriel Synnaeve
abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) on Python execution traces grounds them in code execution and enables the line-by-line execution prediction of whole Python programs, effectively turning them into neural interpreters (FAIR CodeGen Team et al., 2025). However, developers rarely execute programs step by step; instead, they use debuggers to stop execution at certain breakpoints and step through relevant portions only while inspecting or modifying program variables. Existing neural interpreter approaches lack such interactive control. To address this limitation, we introduce neural debuggers: language models that emulate traditional debuggers, supporting operations such as stepping into, over, or out of functions, as well as setting breakpoints at specific source lines. We show that neural debuggers -- obtained via fine-tuning large LLMs or pre-training smaller models from scratch -- can reliably model both forward execution (predicting future states and outputs) and inverse execution (inferring prior states or inputs) conditioned on debugger actions. Evaluated on CruxEval, our models achieve strong performance on both output and input prediction tasks, demonstrating robust conditional execution modeling. Our work takes first steps towards future agentic coding systems in which neural debuggers serve as a world model for simulated debugging environments, providing execution feedback or enabling agents to interact with real debugging tools. This capability lays the foundation for more powerful code generation, program understanding, and automated debugging.
title_zh: 面向Python的神经调试器
abstract_zh: 在Python执行轨迹上训练大型语言模型（LLMs）使其具备代码执行的根基，从而能够对整个Python程序进行逐行执行预测，实质上将这些模型转变为神经解释器（FAIR CodeGen团队等，2025）。然而，开发者很少逐步执行程序；相反，他们通常使用调试器在特定断点处暂停执行，并仅逐步检查或修改相关部分的程序变量。现有的神经解释器方法缺乏这种交互式控制能力。为解决这一局限性，我们提出了“神经调试器”：一种模拟传统调试器的语言模型，支持诸如进入、越过或退出函数，以及在特定源码行设置断点等操作。我们证明，通过微调大型LLM或从头预训练小型模型所获得的神经调试器，能够可靠地建模正向执行（基于调试操作预测未来状态和输出）与逆向执行（基于调试操作推断先前状态或输入）。在CruxEval上的评估显示，我们的模型在输出和输入预测任务上均表现出色，展现出强大的条件执行建模能力。本研究迈出了迈向未来智能编程系统的第一步——其中神经调试器作为模拟调试环境中的世界模型，提供执行反馈，或使智能体能够与真实调试工具进行交互。这一能力为更强大的代码生成、程序理解及自动化调试奠定了基础。

title: Wrong Code, Right Structure: Learning Netlist Representations from Imperfect LLM-Generated RTL
authors: Siyang Cai, Cangyuan Li, Yinhe Han, Ying Wang
abstract: Learning effective netlist representations is fundamentally constrained by the scarcity of labeled datasets, as real designs are protected by Intellectual Property (IP) and costly to annotate. Existing work therefore focuses on small-scale circuits with clean labels, limiting scalability to realistic designs. Meanwhile, Large Language Models (LLMs) can generate Register-Transfer-Level (RTL) at scale, but their functional incorrectness has hindered their use in circuit analysis. In this work, we make a key observation: even when LLM-Generated RTL is functionally imperfect, the synthesized netlists still preserve structural patterns that are strongly indicative of the intended functionality. Building on this insight, we propose a cost-effective data augmentation and training framework that systematically exploits imperfect LLM-Generated RTL as training data for netlist representation learning, forming an end-to-end pipeline from automated code generation to downstream tasks. We conduct evaluations on circuit functional understanding tasks, including sub-circuit boundary identification and component classification, across benchmarks of increasing scales, extending the task scope from operator-level to IP-level. The evaluations demonstrate that models trained on our noisy synthetic corpus generalize well to real-world netlists, matching or even surpassing methods trained on scarce high-quality data and effectively breaking the data bottleneck in circuit representation learning.
title_zh: 错误的代码，正确的结构：从不完美的大模型生成的RTL中学习网表表示
abstract_zh: 学习有效的网表表示从根本上受到标注数据集稀缺性的制约，因为实际电路设计受到知识产权（IP）保护，且标注成本高昂。因此，现有研究主要集中在具有清晰标签的小规模电路上，这限制了其在真实设计中的可扩展性。与此同时，大型语言模型（LLMs）能够大规模生成寄存器传输级（RTL）代码，但其功能上的不准确性阻碍了它们在电路分析中的应用。在本工作中，我们提出一个关键观察：即使LLM生成的RTL在功能上存在缺陷，其综合后的网表仍保留了强烈反映预期功能的结构模式。基于这一洞察，我们提出了一种成本低廉的数据增强与训练框架，系统性地利用这些不完美的LLM生成RTL作为训练数据，用于网表表示学习，构建了一个从自动化代码生成到下游任务的端到端流程。我们在逐步扩大的基准测试中对电路功能理解任务进行了评估，包括子电路边界识别和组件分类，任务范围从操作符级别扩展至IP级别。实验结果表明，使用我们噪声合成语料库训练的模型能够很好地泛化到真实网表，其性能达到甚至超过使用稀有高质量数据训练的方法，有效突破了电路表示学习中的数据瓶颈。

title: Agentic AI as a Network Control-Plane Intelligence Layer for Federated Learning over 6G
authors: Loc X. Nguyen, Ji Su Yoon, Huy Q. Le, Yu Qiao, Avi Deb Raha, Eui-Nam Huh, Nguyen H. Tran, Choong Seon Hong
abstract: The shift toward user-customized on-device learning places new demands on wireless systems: models must be trained on diverse, distributed data while meeting strict latency, bandwidth, and reliability constraints. To address this, we propose an Agentic AI as the control layer for managing federated learning (FL) over 6G networks, which translates high-level task goals into actions that are aware of network conditions. Rather than simply viewing FL as a learning challenge, our system sees it as a combined task of learning and network management. A set of specialized agents focused on retrieval, planning, coding, and evaluation utilizes monitoring tools and optimization methods to handle client selection, incentive structuring, scheduling, resource allocation, adaptive local training, and code generation. The use of closed-loop evaluation and memory allows the system to consistently refine its decisions, taking into account varying signal-to-noise ratios, bandwidth conditions, and device capabilities. Finally, our case study has demonstrated the effectiveness of the Agentic AI system's use of tools for achieving high performance.
title_zh: 面向6G的联邦学习中的智能代理AI作为网络控制面的智能层
abstract_zh: 向用户定制的设备端学习转变，对无线系统提出了新的要求：模型必须在多样化、分布式的数据上进行训练，同时满足严格的延迟、带宽和可靠性约束。为应对这一挑战，我们提出将智能体AI（Agentic AI）作为6G网络中联邦学习（FL）的控制层，能够根据网络状况，将高层次的任务目标转化为具体行动。与仅将联邦学习视为一个学习问题不同，我们的系统将其看作学习与网络管理相结合的综合性任务。一组专注于检索、规划、编码和评估的专用智能体，利用监控工具和优化方法，负责客户端选择、激励机制设计、调度、资源分配、自适应本地训练以及代码生成等关键环节。通过闭环评估与记忆机制，系统能够持续优化决策，充分考虑信号噪声比、带宽条件及设备能力的动态变化。最后，我们的案例研究证明了该智能体AI系统通过有效运用各类工具，在实现高性能方面具有显著成效。

title: Context Engineering: From Prompts to Corporate Multi-Agent Architecture
authors: Vera V. Vishnyakova
abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) systems evolve from stateless chatbots to autonomous multi-step agents, prompt engineering (PE), the discipline of crafting individual queries, proves necessary but insufficient. This paper introduces context engineering (CE) as a standalone discipline concerned with designing, structuring, and managing the entire informational environment in which an AI agent makes decisions. Drawing on vendor architectures (Google ADK, Anthropic, LangChain), current academic work (ACE framework, Google DeepMind's intelligent delegation), enterprise research (Deloitte, 2026; KPMG, 2026), and the author's experience building a multi-agent system, the paper proposes five context quality criteria: relevance, sufficiency, isolation, economy, and provenance, and frames context as the agent's operating system. Two higher-order disciplines follow. Intent engineering (IE) encodes organizational goals, values, and trade-off hierarchies into agent infrastructure. Specification engineering (SE) creates a machine-readable corpus of corporate policies and standards enabling autonomous operation of multi-agent systems at scale. Together these four disciplines form a cumulative pyramid maturity model of agent engineering, in which each level subsumes the previous one as a necessary foundation. Enterprise data reveals a gap: while 75% of enterprises plan agentic AI deployment within two years (Deloitte, 2026), deployment has surged and retreated as organizations confront scaling complexity (KPMG, 2026). The Klarna case illustrates a dual deficit, contextual and intentional. Whoever controls the agent's context controls its behavior; whoever controls its intent controls its strategy; whoever controls its specifications controls its scale.
title_zh: 上下文工程：从提示词到企业级多智能体架构
abstract_zh:
随着人工智能（AI）系统从无状态的聊天机器人演进为自主的多步骤智能体，提示工程（PE）——即设计单个查询的学科——虽仍必要，却已显不足。本文提出“上下文工程”（CE）作为一门独立的学科，专注于设计、构建和管理AI智能体决策所处的整个信息环境。基于主流厂商架构（如Google ADK、Anthropic、LangChain）、当前学术研究（ACE框架、Google DeepMind的智能委派机制）、企业前沿探索（德勤，2026；毕马威，2026），以及作者在构建多智能体系统方面的实践经验，本文提出了五项上下文质量标准：相关性、充分性、隔离性、经济性与可溯源性，并将上下文定义为智能体的“操作系统”。

在此基础上，衍生出两个更高阶的学科：意图工程（IE）负责将组织目标、价值观及权衡优先级编码至智能体基础设施中；规范工程（SE）则致力于创建可机器读取的企业政策与标准语料库，从而实现多智能体系统在规模化场景下的自主运行。

这四项学科共同构成一个递进式的成熟度模型，形成累积式金字塔结构：每一层级均以前一层次为必要基础。企业数据揭示了一个显著差距：尽管75%的企业计划在未来两年内部署代理型AI（德勤，2026），但实际部署却因面临规模化复杂性而出现“爆发—退潮”的反复波动（毕马威，2026）。克劳纳（Klarna）案例凸显了双重缺陷：上下文缺陷与意图缺陷并存。谁掌控智能体的上下文，谁就掌控其行为；谁掌控其意图，谁就掌控其战略；谁掌控其规范，谁就掌控其规模。

title: Avoiding Big Integers: Parallel Multimodular Algebraic Verification of Arithmetic Circuits
authors: Clemens Hofstadler, Daniela Kaufmann, Chen Chen
abstract: Word-level verification of arithmetic circuits with large operands typically relies on arbitrary-precision arithmetic, which can lead to significant computational overhead as word sizes grow. In this paper, we present a hybrid algebraic verification technique based on polynomial reasoning that combines linear and nonlinear rewriting. Our approach relies on multimodular reasoning using homomorphic images, where computations are performed in parallel modulo different primes, thereby avoiding any large-integer arithmetic. We implement the proposed method in the verification tool TalisMan2.0 and evaluate it on a suite of multiplier benchmarks. Our results show that hybrid multimodular reasoning significantly improves upon existing approaches.
title_zh: 避免大整数：算术电路的并行多模代数验证
abstract_zh: 在对具有大操作数的算术电路进行逐词验证时，通常依赖于任意精度算术，这会导致随着字长增加而产生显著的计算开销。本文提出了一种基于多项式推理的混合代数验证技术，该技术结合了线性和非线性重写方法。我们的方法采用基于同态像的多模数推理，即在不同的素数模下并行执行计算，从而完全避免了大整数运算。我们将所提出的算法实现在验证工具TalisMan2.0中，并在一系列乘法器基准测试上进行了评估。实验结果表明，这种混合多模数推理方法显著优于现有的各种方法。

title: EmbC-Test: How to Speed Up Embedded Software Testing Using LLMs and RAG
authors: Maximilian Harnot, Sebastian Komarnicki, Michal Polok, Timo Oksanen
abstract: Manual development of automatic tests for embedded C software is a strenuous and time-consuming task that does not scale well. With the accelerating pace of software release cycles, verification increasingly becomes the bottleneck in the embedded development workflow. This paper presents a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline as a solution for partial automation of the verification process. By grounding a large language model in project-specific artifacts, the approach reduces hallucinations and improves project alignment. An industrial evaluation showed that the generated tests are 100 % syntactically correct, with 85 % successfully passing runtime validation. The proposed solution has the potential to save up to 66 % of the testing time compared to manual test writing while generating 270 tests per hour.
title_zh: EmbC-Test：如何利用大语言模型和RAG加速嵌入式软件测试
abstract_zh: 为嵌入式C软件手动开发自动化测试是一项繁重且耗时的任务，且难以扩展。随着软件发布周期的不断加快，验证环节日益成为嵌入式开发流程中的瓶颈。本文提出了一种检索增强生成（Retrieval-Augmented Generation, RAG）流水线，作为验证过程部分自动化的解决方案。通过将大型语言模型与项目特定的文档资料相结合，该方法有效减少了幻觉现象，提升了与项目的契合度。一项工业级评估表明，生成的测试用例100%语法正确，其中85%能够通过运行时验证。所提出的方案相较于手动编写测试，有望节省高达66%的测试时间，同时每小时可生成270个测试用例。

title: An Analysis of Modern Web Security Vulnerabilities Inside WebAssembly Applications
authors: Lorenzo Corrias, Lorenzo Pisu, Davide Maiorca, Giorgio Giacinto
abstract: The growth in the adoption of the WebAssembly (WASM) standard has given rise to a rapidly increasing landscape of binary applications that are natively ported to the environment of websites. The flexibility of WASM has made it the preferred way to run fast and resource-heavy applications, replacing a field that JavaScript previously monopolized. Despite its success, researchers have raised concerns over the security implementations of WASM, demonstrating that binary vulnerabilities, such as Buffer Overflows and Use After Free, remain a present danger for WASM binaries. Our work aims to demonstrate that such vulnerabilities, when occurring on a WebAssembly module, can affect the behavior of a web application in unexpected ways, enabling an attacker to exploit vulnerabilities that are typical of the web security landscape. We provide several scenarios to provide examples of how each binary vulnerability might lead to a web security vulnerability, such as SQL Injections, XS-Leaks, and SSTI. Our results show that binary vulnerabilities can invalidate common security mechanisms that web developer implement in their applications, demonstrating how the security of WASM modules remains a problem that needs to be addressed. We also provide a list of best practices and defensive strategies that developers can implement to mitigate the risks associated with running unsafe WASM modules in their web applications.
title_zh: WebAssembly 应用程序中现代 Web 安全漏洞分析
abstract_zh:
WebAssembly（WASM）标准的广泛采用，催生了一个快速增长的二进制应用生态，这些应用被原生移植到网站环境中。WASM 的灵活性使其成为运行高速、资源密集型应用的首选方案，逐步取代了此前由 JavaScript 主导的领域。尽管取得了显著成功，研究人员仍对其安全实现表示担忧，指出诸如缓冲区溢出（Buffer Overflow）和使用已释放内存（Use After Free）等二进制漏洞，依然对 WASM 二进制文件构成严重威胁。

我们的研究旨在证明：当此类漏洞出现在 WebAssembly 模块中时，可能以不可预期的方式影响网页应用的行为，从而使攻击者能够利用典型的网络安全隐患进行攻击。我们通过多个具体场景展示了每种二进制漏洞如何演变为网页安全漏洞，例如 SQL 注入、XS-Leaks（跨站点泄露）以及 SSTI（服务器端模板注入）。实验结果表明，这些二进制层面的漏洞可以绕过开发者在应用中部署的常见安全机制，揭示了 WASM 模块的安全问题仍亟待解决。

此外，我们还提出了一套最佳实践与防御策略，供开发者参考，以降低在网页应用中运行不安全 WASM 模块所带来的风险。

title: PrivPRISM: Automatically Detecting Discrepancies Between Google Play Data Safety Declarations and Developer Privacy Policies
authors: Bhanuka Silva, Dishanika Denipitiyage, Anirban Mahanti, Aruna Seneviratne, Suranga Seneviratne
abstract: End-users seldom read verbose privacy policies, leading app stores like Google Play to mandate simplified data safety declarations as a user-friendly alternative. However, these self-declared disclosures often contradict the full privacy policies, deceiving users about actual data practices and violating regulatory requirements for consistency. To address this, we introduce PrivPRISM, a robust framework that combines encoder and decoder language models to systematically extract and compare fine-grained data practices from privacy policies and to compare against data safety declarations, enabling scalable detection of non-compliance. Evaluating 7,770 popular mobile games uncovers discrepancies in nearly 53% of cases, rising to 61% among 1,711 widely used generic apps. Additionally, static code analysis reveals possible under-disclosures, with privacy policies disclosing just 66.8% of potential accesses to sensitive data like location and financial information, versus only 36.4% in data safety declarations of mobile games. Our findings expose systemic issues, including widespread reuse of generic privacy policies, vague / contradictory statements, and hidden risks in high-profile apps with 100M+ downloads, underscoring the urgent need for automated enforcement to protect platform integrity and for end-users to be vigilant about sensitive data they disclose via popular apps.
title_zh: PrivPRISM：自动检测Google Play数据安全声明与开发者隐私政策之间的差异
abstract_zh: 终端用户很少阅读冗长的隐私政策，因此谷歌应用商店等平台要求提供简化的数据安全声明，作为更友好的替代方案。然而，这些由开发者自行申报的声明常常与完整的隐私政策相矛盾，误导用户对实际数据处理行为的认知，并违反了监管机构关于一致性要求的规定。为解决这一问题，我们提出了PrivPRISM——一种强大的框架，结合编码器与解码器语言模型，系统地从隐私政策中提取并比对细粒度的数据实践信息，同时将其与数据安全声明进行对比，从而实现对不合规情况的大规模检测。在对7,770款热门移动游戏的评估中，近53%的案例存在不一致，而在1,711款广泛使用的通用应用程序中，这一比例更是上升至61%。此外，静态代码分析还揭示了潜在的披露不足：隐私政策仅披露了约66.8%的敏感数据（如位置和财务信息）可能访问情况，而移动游戏的数据安全声明中该比例仅为36.4%。我们的研究发现暴露了系统性问题，包括大量通用隐私政策的重复使用、表述模糊或自相矛盾，以及下载量超过千万的知名应用中隐藏的风险。这凸显了迫切需要自动化监管机制以保护平台完整性，同时也提醒终端用户必须警惕通过流行应用所披露的敏感个人信息。

title: TaSR-RAG: Taxonomy-guided Structured Reasoning for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Jiashuo Sun, Yixuan Xie, Jimeng Shi, Shaowen Wang, Jiawei Han
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) helps large language models (LLMs) answer knowledge-intensive and time-sensitive questions by conditioning generation on external evidence. However, most RAG systems still retrieve unstructured chunks and rely on one-shot generation, which often yields redundant context, low information density, and brittle multi-hop reasoning. While structured RAG pipelines can improve grounding, they typically require costly and error-prone graph construction or impose rigid entity-centric structures that do not align with the query's reasoning chain. We propose \textsc{TaSR-RAG}, a taxonomy-guided structured reasoning framework for evidence selection. We represent both queries and documents as relational triples, and constrain entity semantics with a lightweight two-level taxonomy to balance generalization and precision. Given a complex question, \textsc{TaSR-RAG} decomposes it into an ordered sequence of triple sub-queries with explicit latent variables, then performs step-wise evidence selection via hybrid triple matching that combines semantic similarity over raw triples with structural consistency over typed triples. By maintaining an explicit entity binding table across steps, \textsc{TaSR-RAG} resolves intermediate variables and reduces entity conflation without explicit graph construction or exhaustive search. Experiments on multiple multi-hop question answering benchmarks show that \textsc{TaSR-RAG} consistently outperforms strong RAG and structured-RAG baselines by up to 14\%, while producing clearer evidence attribution and more faithful reasoning traces.
title_zh: TaSR-RAG：基于分类体系的结构化推理增强检索生成
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）通过将生成过程基于外部证据，帮助大型语言模型（LLMs）回答知识密集型和时效性强的问题。然而，大多数RAG系统仍依赖于非结构化的文本片段检索，并采用单次生成策略，这常常导致上下文冗余、信息密度低以及脆弱的多跳推理。尽管结构化RAG流程能够提升事实依据的准确性，但它们通常需要成本高昂且易出错的图结构构建，或施加僵化的以实体为中心的结构，难以与查询的实际推理路径对齐。我们提出 \textsc{TaSR-RAG}，一种基于分类体系引导的结构化推理框架，用于证据选择。我们将查询和文档均表示为关系三元组，并利用轻量级两级分类体系约束实体语义，以在泛化能力与精确性之间取得平衡。针对复杂问题，\textsc{TaSR-RAG} 将其分解为一系列带有显式潜在变量的有序三元组子查询，然后通过混合三元组匹配机制进行逐步证据选择：该机制结合了原始三元组间的语义相似性与类型三元组之间的结构一致性。通过在各步骤间维护显式的实体绑定表，\textsc{TaSR-RAG} 能够有效解决中间变量，避免实体混淆，且无需显式图结构构建或穷尽搜索。在多个多跳问答基准测试上的实验表明，\textsc{TaSR-RAG} 在性能上持续优于强大的RAG及结构化RAG基线模型，最高提升达14%，同时生成更清晰的证据归属和更忠实的推理轨迹。

title: ToolRosetta: Bridging Open-Source Repositories and Large Language Model Agents through Automated Tool Standardization
authors: Shimin Di, Xujie Yuan, Hanghui Guo, Chaoqian Ouyang, Zhangze Chen, Ling Yue, Libin Zheng, Jia Zhu, Shaowu Pan, Jian Yin, Min-Ling Zhang, Yong Rui
abstract: Reusing and invoking existing code remains costly and unreliable, as most practical tools are embedded in heterogeneous code repositories and lack standardized, executable interfaces. Although large language models (LLMs) and Model Context Protocol (MCP)-based tool invocation frameworks enable natural language task execution, current approaches rely heavily on manual tool curation and standardization, which fundamentally limits scalability. In this paper, we propose ToolRosetta, a unified framework that automatically translates open-source code repositories and APIs into MCP-compatible tools that can be reliably invoked by LLMs. Given a user task, ToolRosetta autonomously plans toolchains, identifies relevant codebases, and converts them into executable MCP services, enabling end-to-end task completion with minimal human intervention. In addition, ToolRosetta incorporates a security inspection layer to mitigate risks inherent in executing arbitrary code. Extensive experiments across diverse scientific domains demonstrate that ToolRosetta can automatically standardize a large number of open-source tools and reduce the human effort required for code reproduction and deployment. Notably, by seamlessly leveraging specialized open-source tools, ToolRosetta-powered agents consistently improve task completion performance compared to commercial LLMs and existing agent systems.
title_zh: ToolRosetta：通过自动化工具标准化连接开源仓库与大型语言模型代理
abstract_zh: 重用和调用现有代码仍然成本高昂且不可靠，因为大多数实用工具都嵌入在异构的代码仓库中，缺乏标准化、可执行的接口。尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）和基于模型上下文协议（MCP）的工具调用框架能够实现自然语言任务执行，但当前方法仍严重依赖人工进行工具筛选与标准化，从根本上限制了可扩展性。本文提出 ToolRosetta，一个统一的框架，可将开源代码仓库和API自动转换为兼容 MCP 的工具，从而让 LLM 可靠地调用。给定用户任务后，ToolRosetta 能自主规划工具链，识别相关代码库，并将其转化为可执行的 MCP 服务，实现端到端的任务完成，几乎无需人工干预。此外，ToolRosetta 还引入了一层安全检测机制，以降低执行任意代码所带来的风险。在多个科学领域的广泛实验表明，ToolRosetta 能够自动标准化大量开源工具，并显著减少代码复现与部署所需的人力投入。值得注意的是，通过无缝利用专业化的开源工具，基于 ToolRosetta 的智能体在任务完成性能上持续优于商业 LLM 和现有的智能体系统。

title: MM-Zero: Self-Evolving Multi-Model Vision Language Models From Zero Data
authors: Zongxia Li, Hongyang Du, Chengsong Huang, Xiyang Wu, Lantao Yu, Yicheng He, Jing Xie, Xiaomin Wu, Zhichao Liu, Jiarui Zhang, Fuxiao Liu
abstract: Self-evolving has emerged as a key paradigm for improving foundational models such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) with minimal human intervention. While recent approaches have demonstrated that LLM agents can self-evolve from scratch with little to no data, VLMs introduce an additional visual modality that typically requires at least some seed data, such as images, to bootstrap the self-evolution process. In this work, we present Multi-model Multimodal Zero (MM-Zero), the first RL-based framework to achieve zero-data self-evolution for VLM reasoning. Moving beyond prior dual-role (Proposer and Solver) setups, MM-Zero introduces a multi-role self-evolving training framework comprising three specialized roles: a Proposer that generates abstract visual concepts and formulates questions; a Coder that translates these concepts into executable code (e.g., Python, SVG) to render visual images; and a Solver that performs multimodal reasoning over the generated visual content. All three roles are initialized from the same base model and trained using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), with carefully designed reward mechanisms that integrate execution feedback, visual verification, and difficulty balancing. Our experiments show that MM-Zero improves VLM reasoning performance across a wide range of multimodal benchmarks. MM-Zero establishes a scalable path toward self-evolving multi-model systems for multimodal models, extending the frontier of self-improvement beyond the conventional two-model paradigm.
title_zh: MM-Zero：从零数据开始自演化的多模型视觉语言模型
abstract_zh: 自演化已成为提升基础模型（如大语言模型LLMs和视觉语言模型VLMs）的关键范式，能够在极少甚至无需人工干预的情况下实现性能优化。尽管近期方法已证明大语言模型代理可从零开始、几乎不依赖数据地实现自演化，但视觉语言模型引入了额外的视觉模态，通常需要至少一些初始数据（如图像）来启动自演化过程。在本工作中，我们提出了多模态多角色零样本（MM-Zero），这是首个基于强化学习实现VLM推理零数据自演化的框架。与以往双角色（提出者与求解者）设置不同，MM-Zero创新性地构建了一个包含三个专业化角色的多角色自演化训练框架：提出者负责生成抽象的视觉概念并提出问题；编码者将这些概念转化为可执行代码（如Python、SVG），以渲染出可视化图像；求解者则对生成的视觉内容进行多模态推理。这三个角色均从同一基础模型初始化，并通过分组相对策略优化（GRPO）进行训练，同时设计了融合执行反馈、视觉验证及难度平衡的奖励机制。实验结果表明，MM-Zero在多种多模态基准测试中显著提升了VLM的推理能力。MM-Zero为构建可扩展的自演化多模型系统开辟了新路径，将自提升的边界从传统的两模型范式拓展至更复杂的多模态体系。

title: Synergistic Directed Execution and LLM-Driven Analysis for Zero-Day AI-Generated Malware Detection
authors: George Edwards, Mahdi Eslamimehr
abstract: The weaponization of LLMs for automated malware generation poses an existential threat to conventional detection paradigms. AI-generated malware exhibits polymorphic, metamorphic, and context-aware evasion capabilities that render signature-based and shallow heuristic defenses obsolete. This paper introduces a novel hybrid analysis framework that synergistically combines \emph{concolic execution} with \emph{LLM-augmented path prioritization} and \emph{deep-learning-based vulnerability classification} to detect zero-day AI-generated malware with provable guarantees. We formalize the detection problem within a first-order temporal logic over program execution traces, define a lattice-theoretic abstraction for path constraint spaces, and prove both the \emph{soundness} and \emph{relative completeness} of our detection algorithm, assuming classifier correctness. The framework introduces three novel algorithms: (i) an LLM-guided concolic exploration strategy that reduces the average number of explored paths by 73.2\% compared to depth-first search while maintaining equivalent malicious-path coverage; (ii) a transformer-based path-constraint classifier trained on symbolic execution traces; and (iii) a feedback loop that iteratively refines the LLM's prioritization policy using reinforcement learning from detection outcomes. We provide a comprehensive implementation built upon \texttt{angr} 9.2, \texttt{Z3} 4.12, Hugging Face Transformers 4.38, and PyTorch 2.2, with configuration details enabling reproducibility. Experimental evaluation on the EMBER, Malimg, SOREL-20M, and a novel AI-Gen-Malware benchmark comprising 2{,}500 LLM-synthesized samples demonstrates that achieves 98.7\% accuracy on conventional malware and 97.5\% accuracy on AI-generated threats, outperforming ClamAV, YARA, MalConv, and EMBER-GBDT baselines by margins of 8.4--52.2 percentage points on AI-generated samples.
title_zh: 协同定向执行与大模型驱动分析在零日AI生成恶意软件检测中的应用
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLM）被用于自动化生成恶意软件，对传统检测范式构成了生存性威胁。由人工智能生成的恶意软件具备多态性、变形性和上下文感知的逃避能力，使得基于特征签名和浅层启发式防御手段完全失效。本文提出一种新型混合分析框架，通过协同结合**符号化执行**（concolic execution）、**LLM增强的路径优先级规划**以及**基于深度学习的漏洞分类**，实现对零日AI生成恶意软件的检测，并提供可证明的保障。我们将检测问题形式化为程序执行轨迹上的**一阶时序逻辑**，定义了路径约束空间的格论抽象，并在假设分类器正确性的前提下，证明了所提检测算法的**正确性**与**相对完备性**。

该框架引入了三项创新算法：(i) 一种由LLM引导的符号化探索策略，在保持等效恶意路径覆盖率的同时，相比深度优先搜索平均减少了73.2%的路径探索量；(ii) 一个基于Transformer的路径约束分类器，其在符号执行轨迹上进行训练；(iii) 一个反馈循环机制，利用检测结果中的强化学习来迭代优化LLM的路径优先级策略。

我们构建了一个全面的实现系统，基于angr 9.2、Z3 4.12、Hugging Face Transformers 4.38和PyTorch 2.2开发，并提供了完整的配置细节以确保实验可复现性。在EMBER、Malimg、SOREL-20M以及一个包含2,500个LLM合成样本的新颖AI生成恶意软件基准数据集上的实验评估表明，本方法在常规恶意软件上达到98.7%的准确率，在AI生成威胁上达到97.5%的准确率，相较于ClamAV、YARA、MalConv和EMBER-GBDT等基线模型，在AI生成样本上的表现领先8.4至52.2个百分点。

title: CyberThreat-Eval: Can Large Language Models Automate Real-World Threat Research?
authors: Xiangsen Chen, Xuan Feng, Shuo Chen, Matthieu Maitre, Sudipto Rakshit, Diana Duvieilh, Ashley Picone, Nan Tang
abstract: Analyzing Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) from large volumes of data is critical for drafting and publishing comprehensive CTI reports. This process usually follows a three-stage workflow -- triage, deep search and TI drafting. While Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a promising route toward automation, existing benchmarks still have limitations. These benchmarks often consist of tasks that do not reflect real-world analyst workflows. For example, human analysts rarely receive tasks in the form of multiple-choice questions. Also, existing benchmarks often rely on model-centric metrics that emphasize lexical overlap rather than actionable, detailed insights essential for security analysts. Moreover, they typically fail to cover the complete three-stage workflow. To address these issues, we introduce CyberThreat-Eval, which is collected from the daily CTI workflow of a world-leading company. This expert-annotated benchmark assesses LLMs on practical tasks across all three stages as mentioned above. It utilizes analyst-centric metrics that measure factual accuracy, content quality, and operational costs. Our evaluation using this benchmark reveals important insights into the limitations of current LLMs. For example, LLMs often lack the nuanced expertise required to handle complex details and struggle to distinguish between correct and incorrect information. To address these challenges, the CTI workflow incorporates both external ground-truth databases and human expert knowledge. TRA allows human experts to iteratively provide feedback for continuous improvement. The code is available at \href{https://github.com/xschen-beb/CyberThreat-Eval}{\texttt{GitHub}} and \href{https://huggingface.co/datasets/xse/CyberThreat-Eval}{\texttt{HuggingFace}}.
title_zh: CyberThreat-Eval：大型语言模型能否自动化真实世界威胁研究？
abstract_zh: 从海量数据中分析开源情报（OSINT）对于撰写和发布全面的威胁情报（CTI）报告至关重要。这一过程通常遵循三阶段工作流程——初步筛选、深度搜索和情报草案撰写。尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）为自动化提供了有前景的路径，但现有的基准测试仍存在诸多局限。这些基准测试中的任务往往无法反映真实分析师的工作流程：例如，人类分析师极少会以选择题的形式接收任务。此外，现有基准测试多依赖于以词汇重叠为核心的模型中心度量指标，而忽视了安全分析师所关注的可操作性与详尽性洞察。同时，它们通常未能涵盖完整的三阶段工作流程。为解决上述问题，我们提出了 CyberThreat-Eval 基准，该数据集源自一家世界领先的公司的日常CTI工作流程，由专家标注，能够评估LLM在上述三个阶段中实际任务的表现。该基准采用以分析师为中心的度量标准，衡量事实准确性、内容质量以及运营成本。通过该基准进行的评估揭示了当前LLMs的重要局限：例如，LLMs往往缺乏处理复杂细节所需的精细专业知识，且难以区分正确与错误信息。为应对这些挑战，CTI工作流程结合了外部真实数据源和人工专家知识，并引入TRI（迭代反馈）机制，使专家能够持续提供反馈以实现模型的不断优化。相关代码已公开，可在 \href{https://github.com/xschen-beb/CyberThreat-Eval}{\texttt{GitHub}} 和 \href{https://huggingface.co/datasets/xse/CyberThreat-Eval}{\texttt{HuggingFace}} 获取。

title: Idempotent Slices with Applications to Code-Size Reduction
authors: Rafael Alvarenga de Azevedo, Daniel Augusto Costa de Sa, Rodrigo Caetano Rocha, Fernando Magno Quintão Pereira
abstract: Given a value computed within a program, an idempotent backward slice with respect to this value is a maximal subprogram that computes it. An informal notion of an idempotent slice has previously been used by Guimaraes et al. to transform eager into strict evaluation in the LLVM intermediate representation. However, that algorithm is insufficient to be correctly applied to general control-flow graphs. This paper addresses these omissions by formalizing the notion of idempotent backward slices and presenting a sound and efficient algorithm for extracting them from programs in Gated Static Single Assignment (GSA) form. As an example of their practical use, the paper describes how identifying and extracting idempotent backward slices enables a sparse code-size reduction optimization; that is, one capable of merging non-contiguous sequences of instructions within the control-flow graph of a single function or across functions. Experiments with the LLVM test suite show that, in specific benchmarks, this new algorithm achieves code-size reductions up to -7.24% on programs highly optimized by the -Os sequence of passes from clang 17.
title_zh: 幂等切片及其在代码尺寸缩减中的应用
abstract_zh: 在程序中计算得到的一个值，其相对于该值的幂等反向切片（idempotent backward slice）是指能够计算出该值的最大子程序。此前，Guimaraes 等人曾非正式地使用“幂等切片”这一概念，将 LLVM 中间表示中的惰性求值转换为严格求值。然而，该算法无法正确应用于一般的控制流图（control-flow graphs）。本文通过形式化幂等反向切片的概念，并提出一种高效且正确的算法，用于从采用门控静态单赋值（Gated Static Single Assignment, GSA）形式的程序中提取此类切片。作为其实际应用的示例，本文描述了如何通过识别并提取幂等反向切片，实现一种稀疏代码尺寸缩减优化——即能够在单个函数内部或跨函数的控制流图中合并不连续的指令序列。在 LLVM 测试套件上的实验表明，在特定基准测试中，该新算法在 clang 17 的 -Os 优化序列高度优化的程序上，实现了最高达 -7.24% 的代码尺寸缩减。

title: Epistemic Closure: Autonomous Mechanism Completion for Physically Consistent Simulation
authors: Yue Wua, Tianhao Su, Rui Hu, Mingchuan Zhao, Shunbo Hu, Deng Pan, Jizhong Huang
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into scientific discovery is currently hindered by the Implicit Context problem, where governing equations extracted from literature contain invisible thermodynamic assumptions (e.g., undrained conditions) that standard generative models fail to recognize. This leads to Physical Hallucination: the generation of syntactically correct solvers that faithfully execute physically invalid laws. Here, we introduce a Neuro-Symbolic Generative Agent that functions as a cognitive supervisor atop traditional numerical engines. By encapsulating physical laws into modular Constitutive Skills and leveraging latent intrinsic priors, the Agent employs a Chain-of-Thought reasoning workflow to autonomously validate, prune, and complete physical mechanisms. We demonstrate this capability on the challenge of thermal pressurization in low-permeability sandstone. While a standard literature-retrieval baseline erroneously predicts catastrophic material failure by blindly adopting a rigid "undrained" simplification, our Agent autonomously identifies the system as operating in a drained regime (Deborah number De << 1) via dimensionless scaling analysis. Consequently, it inductively completes the missing dissipation mechanism (Darcy flow) required to satisfy boundary constraints, predicting a stable stress path consistent with experimental reality. This work establishes a paradigm where AI agents transcend the role of coding assistants to act as epistemic partners, capable of reasoning about and correcting the theoretical assumptions embedded in scientific data.
title_zh: 认识闭包：用于物理一致模拟的自主机制完备性
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在科学发现中的应用目前受到“隐含上下文”问题的制约：从文献中提取的控制方程往往包含不可见的热力学假设（如无排水条件），而标准生成模型无法识别这些假设。这导致了“物理幻觉”现象——生成语法正确但物理上无效的求解器，其执行的是违背物理规律的算法。本文提出一种神经符号生成代理（Neuro-Symbolic Generative Agent），作为传统数值引擎之上的认知监督者。该代理将物理定律封装为模块化的本构技能（Constitutive Skills），并利用潜在的内在先验知识，通过思维链（Chain-of-Thought）推理流程，自主验证、剪枝并补全物理机制。我们在低渗透砂岩中热压致密化这一挑战性问题上验证了该方法的有效性。与仅依赖文献检索的基线方法错误地因盲目采用刚性“无排水”简化假设而预测灾难性材料失效不同，我们的代理通过无量纲尺度分析，自主识别出系统处于排水状态（德博拉数 De << 1）。由此，它归纳性地补全了缺失的能量耗散机制（达西流），以满足边界约束条件，从而预测出与实验现实一致的稳定应力路径。本研究建立了一种新范式：AI代理不再局限于代码助手的角色，而是成为能够推理并修正科学数据中嵌入理论假设的智识伙伴。

title: Preparing Students for AI-Driven Agile Development: A Project-Based AI Engineering Curriculum
authors: Andreas Rausch, Stefan Wittek, Tobias Geger, David Inkermann
abstract: Generative AI and agentic tools are reshaping agile software development, yet many engineering curricula still teach agile methods and AI competencies separately and largely lecture-based. This paper presents a project-based AI Engineering curriculum designed to prepare students for AI-driven agile development by integrating agile practices and AI-enabled engineering throughout the program. We contribute (1) the curriculum concept and guiding principles, (2) a case study of interdisciplinary, AI-enabled agile student projects, and (3) early evidence from a mixed-methods evaluation. In our case study, second-semester bachelor students work in teams over seven two-week sprints on a realistic software product. AI tools are embedded into everyday agile engineering tasks - requirements clarification, backlog refinement, architectural reasoning, coding support, testing, and documentation - paired with reflection on human responsibility and quality. Initial results indicate that the integrated approach supports hands-on competence development in AI-assisted engineering. Key observations highlight the need for agile teaching adaptations due to rapid tool evolution, the critical role of oral verification to ensure foundational learning. We close with lessons learned and recommendations for educators designing agile project-based curricula in the age of AI.
title_zh: 面向人工智能驱动的敏捷开发：基于项目的AI工程课程体系
abstract_zh: 生成式人工智能与代理型工具正在重塑敏捷软件开发，然而许多工程教育课程仍以分离且主要采用讲座形式的方式教授敏捷方法与AI技能。本文提出了一种基于项目的AI工程课程体系，旨在通过在整个课程中融合敏捷实践与AI赋能的工程能力，帮助学生为AI驱动的敏捷开发做好准备。本文贡献包括：（1）课程理念与指导原则；（2）跨学科、AI赋能的敏捷学生项目案例研究；（3）来自混合方法评估的初步证据。在案例研究中，二年级本科生以团队形式，在为期七轮、每轮两周的迭代周期内，针对一个真实的软件产品开展开发工作。AI工具被嵌入日常的敏捷工程任务中——包括需求澄清、待办事项优化、架构设计推理、编码支持、测试以及文档编写，并结合对人类责任与质量保障的反思。初步结果表明，这种整合式教学方法有效促进了学生在AI辅助工程方面的实践能力发展。关键观察指出，由于工具快速演进，敏捷教学亟需相应调整；同时，口头验证在确保基础学习效果方面具有关键作用。最后，本文总结了经验教训，并为在AI时代设计敏捷项目制课程的教育工作者提出了具体建议。

title: Beyond Test-Time Training: Learning to Reason via Hardware-Efficient Optimal Control
authors: Peihao Wang, Shan Yang, Xijun Wang, Tesi Xiao, Xin Liu, Changlong Yu, Yu Lou, Pan Li, Zhangyang Wang, Ming Lin, René Vidal
abstract: Associative memory has long underpinned the design of sequential models. Beyond recall, humans reason by projecting future states and selecting goal-directed actions, a capability that modern language models increasingly require but do not natively encode. While prior work uses reinforcement learning or test-time training, planning remains external to the model architecture. We formulate reasoning as optimal control and introduce the Test-Time Control (TTC) layer, which performs finite-horizon LQR planning over latent states at inference time, represents a value function within neural architectures, and leverages it as the nested objective to enable planning before prediction. To ensure scalability, we derive a hardware-efficient LQR solver based on a symplectic formulation and implement it as a fused CUDA kernel, enabling parallel execution with minimal overhead. Integrated as an adapter into pretrained LLMs, TTC layers improve mathematical reasoning performance by up to +27.8% on MATH-500 and 2-3x Pass@8 improvements on AMC and AIME, demonstrating that embedding optimal control as an architectural component provides an effective and scalable mechanism for reasoning beyond test-time training.
title_zh: 超越测试时训练：通过硬件高效最优控制来学习推理
abstract_zh: 关联记忆长期以来一直是序列模型设计的基础。除了记忆召回之外，人类还通过预测未来状态并选择目标导向的行为来进行推理，而现代语言模型日益需要这种能力，却并未原生地具备。尽管先前的研究采用了强化学习或测试时训练的方法，但规划仍被置于模型架构之外。我们提出将推理建模为最优控制问题，并引入测试时控制（Test-Time Control, TTC）层：该层在推理阶段对潜在状态执行有限时域的线性二次调节器（LQR）规划，将价值函数嵌入神经网络架构中，并利用其作为嵌套目标，在预测之前实现规划。为确保可扩展性，我们基于辛几何形式推导出一种硬件高效的LQR求解器，并将其实现为融合的CUDA内核，从而实现并行执行且开销极小。将TTC层作为适配器集成到预训练的大语言模型中后，其在MATH-500上的数学推理性能提升高达27.8%，在AMC和AIME上实现Pass@8指标2至3倍的提升，表明将最优控制作为架构组件嵌入，是一种有效且可扩展的推理机制，超越了传统的测试时训练方法。

title: AgenticCyOps: Securing Multi-Agentic AI Integration in Enterprise Cyber Operations
authors: Shaswata Mitra, Raj Patel, Sudip Mittal, Md Rayhanur Rahman, Shahram Rahimi
abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) powered by LLMs promise adaptive, reasoning-driven enterprise workflows, yet granting agents autonomous control over tools, memory, and communication introduces attack surfaces absent from deterministic pipelines. While current research largely addresses prompt-level exploits and narrow individual vectors, it lacks a holistic architectural model for enterprise-grade security. We introduce AgenticCyOps (Securing Multi-Agentic AI Integration in Enterprise Cyber Operations), a framework built on a systematic decomposition of attack surfaces across component, coordination, and protocol layers, revealing that documented vectors consistently trace back to two integration surfaces: tool orchestration and memory management. Building on this observation, we formalize these integration surfaces as primary trust boundaries and define five defensive principles: authorized interfaces, capability scoping, verified execution, memory integrity & synchronization, and access-controlled data isolation; each aligned with established compliance standards (NIST, ISO 27001, GDPR, EU AI Act). We apply the framework to a Security Operations Center (SOC) workflow, adopting the Model Context Protocol (MCP) as the structural basis, with phase-scoped agents, consensus validation loops, and per-organization memory boundaries. Coverage analysis, attack path tracing, and trust boundary assessment confirm that the design addresses the documented attack vectors with defense-in-depth, intercepts three of four representative attack chains within the first two steps, and reduces exploitable trust boundaries by a minimum of 72% compared to a flat MAS, positioning AgenticCyOps as a foundation for securing enterprise-grade integration.
title_zh: 智能体CyOps：保障企业网络运营中多智能体AI集成的安全
abstract_zh: 由大语言模型（LLM）驱动的多智能体系统（MAS）有望实现自适应、基于推理的企业级工作流，然而赋予智能体对工具、记忆和通信的自主控制权，也引入了传统确定性流程中不存在的安全攻击面。尽管当前研究主要聚焦于提示层攻击与特定个体攻击向量，却缺乏面向企业级安全的系统性架构模型。本文提出AgenticCyOps（企业级网络安全运营中的多智能体AI集成安全框架），该框架通过系统性分解组件层、协调层与协议层的攻击面，揭示出已知攻击向量始终可追溯至两个核心集成界面：工具编排与记忆管理。基于此发现，我们将这两个集成界面形式化为关键信任边界，并定义五大防御原则：授权接口、能力范围限定、执行验证、记忆完整性与同步机制，以及受控访问的数据隔离；所有原则均与主流合规标准（NIST、ISO 27001、GDPR、欧盟人工智能法案）保持一致。我们将该框架应用于安全运营中心（SOC）工作流，以模型上下文协议（MCP）为结构基础，引入阶段化智能体、共识验证循环及组织级独立记忆边界。覆盖度分析、攻击路径追踪与信任边界评估结果表明，该设计通过纵深防御策略有效应对已知攻击向量，在四条代表性攻击链中前两步即拦截其中三条，且相比扁平化多智能体系统，可将可被利用的信任边界减少至少72%。AgenticCyOps因此成为保障企业级智能体集成安全的坚实基础。

title: EPOCH: An Agentic Protocol for Multi-Round System Optimization
authors: Zhanlin Liu, Yitao Li, Munirathnam Srikanth
abstract: Autonomous agents are increasingly used to improve prompts, code, and machine learning systems through iterative execution and feedback. Yet existing approaches are usually designed as task-specific optimization loops rather than as a unified protocol for establishing baselines and managing tracked multi-round self-improvement. We introduce EPOCH, an engineering protocol for multi-round system optimization in heterogeneous environments. EPOCH organizes optimization into two phases: baseline construction and iterative self-improvement. It further structures each round through role-constrained stages that separate planning, implementation, and evaluation, and standardizes execution through canonical command interfaces and round-level tracking. This design enables coordinated optimization across prompts, model configurations, code, and rule-based components while preserving stability, reproducibility, traceability, and integrity of evaluation. Empirical studies in various tasks illustrate the practicality of EPOCH for production-oriented autonomous improvement workflows.
title_zh: EPOCH：一种用于多轮系统优化的智能体协议
abstract_zh: 自主代理正越来越多地通过迭代执行和反馈来提升提示词、代码及机器学习系统。然而，现有方法通常被设计为特定任务的优化循环，而非统一的协议，难以建立基准并管理多轮自改进过程。我们提出了 EPOCH，一种用于异构环境中多轮系统优化的工程协议。EPOCH 将优化过程分为两个阶段：基线构建与迭代自改进。它进一步通过角色约束的阶段结构，将每一轮划分为规划、实现和评估三个独立环节，并通过标准的命令接口和轮次级追踪机制规范执行流程。这一设计使得在提示词、模型配置、代码以及基于规则的组件之间实现协同优化成为可能，同时保持了系统的稳定性、可复现性、可追溯性与评估完整性。在多种任务中的实证研究证明，EPOCH 在面向生产环境的自主改进工作流中具有高度实用性。

title: Thinking to Recall: How Reasoning Unlocks Parametric Knowledge in LLMs
authors: Zorik Gekhman, Roee Aharoni, Eran Ofek, Mor Geva, Roi Reichart, Jonathan Herzig
abstract: While reasoning in LLMs plays a natural role in math, code generation, and multi-hop factual questions, its effect on simple, single-hop factual questions remains unclear. Such questions do not require step-by-step logical decomposition, making the utility of reasoning highly counterintuitive. Nevertheless, we find that enabling reasoning substantially expands the capability boundary of the model's parametric knowledge recall, unlocking correct answers that are otherwise effectively unreachable. Why does reasoning aid parametric knowledge recall when there are no complex reasoning steps to be done? To answer this, we design a series of hypothesis-driven controlled experiments, and identify two key driving mechanisms: (1) a computational buffer effect, where the model uses the generated reasoning tokens to perform latent computation independent of their semantic content; and (2) factual priming, where generating topically related facts acts as a semantic bridge that facilitates correct answer retrieval. Importantly, this latter generative self-retrieval mechanism carries inherent risks: we demonstrate that hallucinating intermediate facts during reasoning increases the likelihood of hallucinations in the final answer. Finally, we show that our insights can be harnessed to directly improve model accuracy by prioritizing reasoning trajectories that contain hallucination-free factual statements.
title_zh: 思维回溯：推理如何解锁大语言模型中的参数化知识
abstract_zh: 尽管在数学、代码生成以及多跳事实类问题中，大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力发挥着自然作用，但其对简单的一跳式事实类问题的影响仍不明确。这类问题无需逐步的逻辑分解，因此推理能力的效用显得极不直观。然而，我们发现开启推理机制显著拓展了模型参数化知识回忆的能力边界，解锁了原本几乎无法获取的正确答案。那么，当问题本身并不需要复杂推理步骤时，为何推理仍能促进参数化知识的召回？为解答这一问题，我们设计了一系列基于假设的受控实验，识别出两个关键驱动机制：（1）计算缓冲效应，即模型利用生成的推理标记进行隐式的计算，而这些标记的语义内容无关紧要；（2）事实提示效应，即生成与主题相关的事实可作为语义桥梁，促进正确答案的检索。值得注意的是，这种生成式自我检索机制本身存在内在风险：我们证明，在推理过程中虚构中间事实会显著增加最终答案出现幻觉的可能性。最后，我们展示了如何利用这些洞见直接提升模型的准确性——通过优先选择包含无幻觉事实陈述的推理路径。

title: Good Reasoning Makes Good Demonstrations: Implicit Reasoning Quality Supervision via In-Context Reinforcement Learning
authors: Tiehua Mei, Minxuan Lv, Leiyu Pan, Zhenpeng Su, Hongru Hou, Hengrui Chen, Ao Xu, Deqing Yang
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) improves reasoning in large language models but treats all correct solutions equally, potentially reinforcing flawed traces that get correct answers by chance. We observe that better reasoning are better teachers: high-quality solutions serve as more effective demonstrations than low-quality ones. We term this teaching ability Demonstration Utility, and show that the policy model's own in-context learning ability provides an efficient way to measure it, yielding a quality signal termed Evidence Gain. To employ this signal during training, we introduce In-Context RLVR. By Bayesian analysis, we show that this objective implicitly reweights rewards by Evidence Gain, assigning higher weights to high-quality traces and lower weights to low-quality ones, without requiring costly computation or external evaluators. Experiments on mathematical benchmarks show improvements in both accuracy and reasoning quality over standard RLVR.
title_zh: 良好的推理带来优秀的演示：通过上下文强化学习实现隐式推理质量监督
abstract_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）能够提升大语言模型的推理能力，但其对所有正确解法一视同仁，可能无意中强化了那些偶然得到正确答案却存在缺陷的推理过程。我们观察到：更优的推理过程是更好的“教师”——高质量的解法作为示范比低质量的解法更具教学效果。我们将这种教学能力称为“示范效用”（Demonstration Utility），并发现策略模型自身的上下文学习能力可高效衡量该效用，从而生成一种称为“证据增益”（Evidence Gain）的质量信号。为在训练中利用这一信号，我们提出了“上下文内RLVR”（In-Context RLVR）。通过贝叶斯分析，我们证明该目标函数隐式地根据证据增益对奖励进行重加权：高质推理路径获得更高权重，低质路径则获得较低权重，且无需额外计算开销或依赖外部评估器。在数学基准测试上的实验表明，与标准RLVR相比，该方法在准确率和推理质量上均取得显著提升。

title: EsoLang-Bench: Evaluating Genuine Reasoning in Large Language Models via Esoteric Programming Languages
authors: Aman Sharma, Paras Chopra
abstract: Large language models achieve near-ceiling performance on code generation benchmarks, yet these results increasingly reflect memorization rather than genuine reasoning. We introduce EsoLang-Bench, a benchmark using five esoteric programming languages (Brainfuck, Befunge-98, Whitespace, Unlambda, and Shakespeare) that lack benchmark gaming incentives due to their economic irrationality for pre-training. These languages require the same computational primitives as mainstream programming but have 1,000-100,000x fewer public repositories than Python (based on GitHub search counts). We evaluate five frontier models across five prompting strategies and find a dramatic capability gap: models achieving 85-95% on standard benchmarks score only 0-11% on equivalent esoteric tasks, with 0% accuracy beyond the Easy tier. Few-shot learning and self-reflection fail to improve performance, suggesting these techniques exploit training priors rather than enabling genuine learning. EsoLang-Bench provides the first benchmark designed to mimic human learning by acquiring new languages through documentation, interpreter feedback, and iterative experimentation, measuring transferable reasoning skills resistant to data contamination.
title_zh: EsoLang-Bench：通过晦涩编程语言评估大语言模型的真实推理能力
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型在代码生成基准测试中已达到接近上限的性能，然而这些结果越来越多地反映出的是记忆而非真正的推理能力。我们提出了 EsoLang-Bench，这是一个基于五种冷门编程语言（Brainfuck、Befunge-98、Whitespace、Unlambda 和 Shakespeare）的基准测试。由于这些语言在预训练中缺乏经济合理性，因此不会诱发“基准游戏”行为。这些语言与主流编程语言具有相同的计算原语，但其公开仓库数量仅为 Python 的 1,000 到 100,000 倍之间（基于 GitHub 搜索统计）。我们在五种前沿模型上评估了五种不同的提示策略，发现能力差距极为显著：在标准基准测试中得分达 85%-95% 的模型，在对应冷门语言任务上的表现仅能达到 0%-11%，且在“Easy”难度级别之外准确率降为 0%。少样本学习和自我反思等方法均未能提升性能，表明这些技术依赖的是训练数据中的先验偏好，而非真正意义上的学习能力。EsoLang-Bench 是首个旨在模拟人类学习过程的基准测试——通过文档阅读、解释器反馈和迭代实验来掌握新语言，从而衡量那些不受数据污染影响的可迁移推理能力。

title: Learning Adaptive LLM Decoding
authors: Chloe H. Su, Zhe Ye, Samuel Tenka, Aidan Yang, Soonho Kong, Udaya Ghai
abstract: Decoding from large language models (LLMs) typically relies on fixed sampling hyperparameters (e.g., temperature, top-p), despite substantial variation in task difficulty and uncertainty across prompts and individual decoding steps. We propose to learn adaptive decoding policies that dynamically select sampling strategies at inference time, conditioned on available compute resources. Rather than fine-tuning the language model itself, we introduce lightweight decoding adapters trained with reinforcement learning and verifiable terminal rewards (e.g. correctness on math and coding tasks). At the sequence level, we frame decoding as a contextual bandit problem: a policy selects a decoding strategy (e.g. greedy, top-k, min-p) for each prompt, conditioned on the prompt embedding and a parallel sampling budget. At the token level, we model decoding as a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP), where a policy selects sampling actions at each token step based on internal model features and the remaining token budget. Experiments on the MATH and CodeContests benchmarks show that the learned adapters improve the accuracy-budget tradeoff: on MATH, the token-level adapter improves Pass@1 accuracy by up to 10.2% over the best static baseline under a fixed token budget, while the sequence-level adapter yields 2-3% gains under fixed parallel sampling. Ablation analyses support the contribution of both sequence- and token-level adaptation.
title_zh: 学习自适应的LLM解码
abstract_zh: 从大型语言模型（LLMs）中解码通常依赖于固定的采样超参数（如温度、top-p），尽管不同提示以及每个解码步骤之间的任务难度和不确定性存在显著差异。我们提出学习自适应的解码策略，能够在推理时动态选择采样方法，并根据可用的计算资源进行调整。与微调语言模型本身不同，我们引入了轻量级的解码适配器，通过强化学习和可验证的终端奖励（例如数学和编程任务中的正确性）进行训练。在序列层面，我们将解码建模为一个上下文相关的老虎机问题：策略根据提示嵌入和并行采样预算，为每个提示选择一种解码策略（如贪婪、top-k、min-p）。在标记层面，我们将解码建模为一个部分可观测的马尔可夫决策过程（POMDP），其中策略基于模型内部特征和剩余的标记预算，在每一步标记生成时选择采样动作。在MATH和CodeContests基准上的实验表明，所学适配器能够改善准确率与预算之间的权衡：在MATH数据集上，标记级适配器在固定标记预算下，将Pass@1准确率相比最佳静态基线最高提升了10.2%；而序列级适配器在固定并行采样条件下也实现了2-3%的提升。消融分析进一步验证了序列级和标记级自适应机制各自的贡献。

title: Reward Prediction with Factorized World States
authors: Yijun Shen, Delong Chen, Xianming Hu, Jiaming Mi, Hongbo Zhao, Kai Zhang, Pascale Fung
abstract: Agents must infer action outcomes and select actions that maximize a reward signal indicating how close the goal is to being reached. Supervised learning of reward models could introduce biases inherent to training data, limiting generalization to novel goals and environments. In this paper, we investigate whether well-defined world state representations alone can enable accurate reward prediction across domains. To address this, we introduce StateFactory, a factorized representation method that transforms unstructured observations into a hierarchical object-attribute structure using language models. This structured representation allows rewards to be estimated naturally as the semantic similarity between the current state and the goal state under hierarchical constraint. Overall, the compact representation structure induced by StateFactory enables strong reward generalization capabilities. We evaluate on RewardPrediction, a new benchmark dataset spanning five diverse domains and comprising 2,454 unique action-observation trajectories with step-wise ground-truth rewards. Our method shows promising zero-shot results against both VLWM-critic and LLM-as-a-Judge reward models, achieving 60% and 8% lower EPIC distance, respectively. Furthermore, this superior reward quality successfully translates into improved agent planning performance, yielding success rate gains of +21.64% on AlfWorld and +12.40% on ScienceWorld over reactive system-1 policies and enhancing system-2 agent planning. Project Page: https://statefactory.github.io
title_zh: 基于因子化世界状态的奖励预测
abstract_zh: 代理必须推断行动结果，并选择能够最大化奖励信号的动作，该信号反映了目标的接近程度。通过监督学习训练奖励模型可能会引入训练数据固有的偏差，从而限制其在新目标和新环境中的泛化能力。本文探讨了仅依靠良好定义的世界状态表示是否足以在不同领域实现准确的奖励预测。为此，我们提出了StateFactory，一种基于语言模型的因子化表示方法，可将非结构化的观测信息转化为分层的对象-属性结构。这种结构化表示使得奖励能够自然地被估计为当前状态与目标状态之间在层次约束下的语义相似性。总体而言，StateFactory所生成的紧凑表示结构赋予了强大的奖励泛化能力。我们在一个名为RewardPrediction的新基准数据集上进行了评估，该数据集涵盖五个不同的领域，包含2,454条独特的动作-观测轨迹，每一步均有真实奖励标签。我们的方法在零样本设置下表现出色，相较于VLWM-critic和LLM-as-a-Judge奖励模型，分别实现了60%和8%更低的EPIC距离。此外，这种优越的奖励质量成功转化为更优的智能体规划性能，在AlfWorld上相比反应式系统-1策略提升了21.64%的成功率，在ScienceWorld上提升了12.40%，显著增强了系统-2智能体的规划能力。项目页面：https://statefactory.github.io

title: Explainable Innovation Engine: Dual-Tree Agent-RAG with Methods-as-Nodes and Verifiable Write-Back
authors: Renwei Meng
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) improves factual grounding, yet most systems rely on flat chunk retrieval and provide limited control over multi-step synthesis. We propose an Explainable Innovation Engine that upgrades the knowledge unit from text chunks to methods-as-nodes. The engine maintains a weighted method provenance tree for traceable derivations and a hierarchical clustering abstraction tree for efficient top-down navigation. At inference time, a strategy agent selects explicit synthesis operators (e.g., induction, deduction, analogy), composes new method nodes, and records an auditable trajectory. A verifier-scorer layer then prunes low-quality candidates and writes validated nodes back to support continual growth. Expert evaluation across six domains and multiple backbones shows consistent gains over a vanilla baseline, with the largest improvements on derivation-heavy settings, and ablations confirm the complementary roles of provenance backtracking and pruning. These results suggest a practical path toward controllable, explainable, and verifiable innovation in agentic RAG systems. Code is available at the project GitHub repository https://github.com/xiaolu-666113/Dual-Tree-Agent-RAG.
title_zh: 可解释性创新引擎：基于方法为节点的双树代理-RAG 与可验证写回机制
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）提升了事实性基础，但大多数系统仍依赖于扁平的文本块检索，对多步合成过程的控制能力有限。我们提出了一种可解释的创新引擎，将知识单元从传统的文本块升级为“方法即节点”的形式。该引擎维护一个加权的方法溯源树，实现可追溯的推导过程，并构建了一个分层聚类抽象树，支持高效的自顶向下导航。在推理阶段，策略代理会选择明确的合成操作（如归纳、演绎、类比），组合生成新的方法节点，并记录可审计的推理轨迹。随后，验证-评分模块会剔除低质量候选节点，并将经过验证的节点写回系统，以支持持续的知识增长。在六个不同领域及多种模型架构上的专家评估表明，该方法在所有设置下均显著优于基线模型，尤其在依赖复杂推导的任务中表现突出；消融实验进一步证实了溯源回溯与剪枝机制之间的互补作用。这些结果表明，本方法为实现可控、可解释且可验证的智能体式RAG系统中的创新提供了一条切实可行的路径。代码已开源至项目GitHub仓库：https://github.com/xiaolu-666113/Dual-Tree-Agent-RAG。

title: Understanding the Interplay between LLMs' Utilisation of Parametric and Contextual Knowledge: A keynote at ECIR 2025
authors: Isabelle Augenstein
abstract: Language Models (LMs) acquire parametric knowledge from their training process, embedding it within their weights. The increasing scalability of LMs, however, poses significant challenges for understanding a model's inner workings and further for updating or correcting this embedded knowledge without the significant cost of retraining. Moreover, when using these language models for knowledge-intensive language understanding tasks, LMs have to integrate relevant context, mitigating their inherent weaknesses, such as incomplete or outdated knowledge. Nevertheless, studies indicate that LMs often ignore the provided context as it can be in conflict with the pre-existing LM's memory learned during pre-training. Conflicting knowledge can also already be present in the LM's parameters, termed intra-memory conflict. This underscores the importance of understanding the interplay between how a language model uses its parametric knowledge and the retrieved contextual knowledge. In this talk, I will aim to shed light on this important issue by presenting our research on evaluating the knowledge present in LMs, diagnostic tests that can reveal knowledge conflicts, as well as on understanding the characteristics of successfully used contextual knowledge.
title_zh: 大型语言模型中参数化知识与上下文知识相互作用的探究：2025年ECIR大会主题演讲
abstract_zh: 语言模型（LMs）在其训练过程中获取参数化知识，并将其嵌入到模型的权重之中。然而，随着语言模型规模的不断扩展，理解模型内部工作机制变得愈发困难，同时在不付出高昂重训练成本的情况下更新或修正这些嵌入知识也面临巨大挑战。此外，在执行需要大量知识的语言理解任务时，语言模型必须整合相关上下文信息，以弥补其固有的缺陷，例如知识不完整或过时的问题。然而，研究表明，当提供的上下文与预训练阶段习得的模型记忆存在冲突时，语言模型往往选择忽略这些上下文信息。这种冲突甚至可能已经存在于模型参数中，被称为“内部记忆冲突”。这凸显了理解语言模型如何利用其参数化知识与检索到的上下文知识之间相互作用的重要性。在本次报告中，我将通过介绍我们在评估语言模型中所蕴含知识、诊断可揭示知识冲突的测试方法，以及对成功使用上下文知识特征的理解等方面的研究工作，来深入探讨这一重要问题。

title: Compartmentalization-Aware Automated Program Repair
authors: Jia Hu, Youcheng Sun, Pierre Olivier
abstract: Software compartmentalization breaks down an application into compartments isolated from each other: an attacker taking over a compartment will be confined to it, limiting the damage they can cause to the rest of the application. Despite the security promises of this approach, recent studies have shown that most existing compartmentalized software is plagued by vulnerabilities at cross-compartment interfaces, allowing an attacker taking over a compartment to escape its confinement and negate the security guarantees expected from compartmentalization. In that context, securing cross-compartment interfaces is notoriously difficult and engineering-intensive. In light of recent advances in Automated Program Repair (APR), notably through the use of Large Language Models (LLMs), this paper presents a work in progress investigating the suitability of LLM-based APR at securing cross-compartment interfaces as automatically as possible. We observe that existing APR approaches and general purpose/code-centric LLMs used as is are unfit for this task, and present the design, implementation, and early results of a new APR framework dedicated to compartment interface safety. The framework integrates into a feedback loop 1) a specialized fuzzer uncovering cross-compartment interface vulnerabilities; 2) a patch generation component bridging the lack of compartmentalization awareness of existing LLMs with a series of analysis techniques; and 3) a patch validation component assessing the effectiveness of generated vulnerability fixes. We validate our framework over a sample interface vulnerability, comparing it to a naive use of general-purpose LLMs, and discuss future research avenues.
title_zh: 基于隔间感知的自动化程序修复
abstract_zh: 软件分隔技术将应用程序分解为彼此隔离的独立单元：一旦攻击者攻陷某个单元，其活动将被限制在该单元内，从而最大限度地减少对应用程序其他部分造成的损害。尽管这种技术具有显著的安全潜力，但近期研究表明，大多数现有的分隔式软件在跨单元接口处存在严重漏洞，使得攻击者能够突破其所在单元的隔离限制，从而完全抵消分隔化带来的安全保障。因此，确保跨单元接口的安全性历来极为困难且工程成本高昂。鉴于自动程序修复（APR）领域近年来取得的进展，特别是大型语言模型（LLMs）的应用，本文介绍了一项正在进行的研究工作，旨在探索基于LLM的APR技术在自动化程度上保障跨单元接口安全的可行性。我们发现，现有的APR方法以及直接使用的通用型/代码导向型LLMs并不适合这一任务，并提出了一个专为保障单元接口安全而设计的新APR框架的设计、实现及初步实验结果。该框架构建于一个反馈循环中，包含三个核心组件：1）专用模糊测试工具，用于发现跨单元接口中的潜在漏洞；2）补丁生成模块，通过一系列分析技术弥补现有LLMs缺乏对分隔化机制认知的缺陷；3）补丁验证模块，用于评估所生成修复方案的有效性。我们在一个典型的接口漏洞案例上验证了该框架的性能，并与直接使用通用LLM的简单方法进行了对比，最后探讨了未来的研究方向。

title: Efficiently Aligning Draft Models via Parameter- and Data-Efficient Adaptation
authors: Luxi Lin, Zhihang Lin, Zhanpeng Zeng, Yuhao Chen, Qingyu Zhang, Jixiang Luo, Xuelong Li, Rongrong Ji
abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference but suffers from performance degradation when target models are fine-tuned for specific domains. A naive solution is to retrain draft models for every target model, which is costly and inefficient. To address this, we introduce a parameter- and data-efficient framework named Efficient Draft Adaptation, abbreviated as EDA, for efficiently adapting draft models. EDA introduces three innovations: (1) a decoupled architecture that utilizes shared and private components to model the shared and target-specific output distributions separately, enabling parameter-efficient adaptation by updating only the lightweight private component;(2) a data regeneration strategy that utilizes the fine-tuned target model to regenerate training data, thereby improving the alignment between training and speculative decoding, leading to higher average acceptance length;(3) a sample selection mechanism that prioritizes high-value data for efficient adaptation. Our experiments show that EDA effectively restores speculative performance on fine-tuned models, achieving superior average acceptance lengths with significantly reduced training costs compared to full retraining. Code is available at https://github.com/Lyn-Lucy/Efficient-Draft-Adaptation.
title_zh: 通过参数高效与数据高效适配实现草稿模型的高效对齐
abstract_zh: 推测解码能够加速大语言模型（LLM）的推理过程，但在目标模型经过特定领域微调后，其性能往往会下降。一种简单的解决方案是为每个目标模型重新训练对应的草稿模型（draft model），但这代价高昂且效率低下。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种参数和数据高效的框架——高效草稿适配（Efficient Draft Adaptation，简称 EDA），用于高效地适应草稿模型。EDA引入了三项创新：（1）解耦架构：通过共享组件与私有组件分别建模通用输出分布和目标特定输出分布，仅需更新轻量级的私有组件即可实现参数高效的适配；（2）数据再生策略：利用微调后的目标模型生成新的训练数据，从而增强训练数据与推测解码之间的对齐性，显著提升平均接受长度；（3）样本选择机制：优先选取高价值样本进行训练，以实现更高效的适配。实验结果表明，EDA能有效恢复微调后模型上的推测解码性能，在获得更优平均接受长度的同时，相比全量重训大幅降低了训练成本。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/Lyn-Lucy/Efficient-Draft-Adaptation。

title: Quantifying the Necessity of Chain of Thought through Opaque Serial Depth
authors: Jonah Brown-Cohen, David Lindner, Rohin Shah
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) tend to externalize their reasoning in their chain of thought, making the chain of thought a good target for monitoring. This is partially an inherent feature of the Transformer architecture: sufficiently long serial cognition must pass through the chain of thought (Korbak et al., 2025). We formalize this argument through the notion of opaque serial depth, given by the length of the longest computation that can be done without the use of interpretable intermediate steps like chain of thought. Given this formalization, we compute numeric upper bounds on the opaque serial depth of Gemma 3 models, as well as asymptotic results for additional architectures beyond standard LLMs. We also open-source an automated method that can calculate upper bounds on the opaque serial depth of arbitrary neural networks, and use it to demonstrate that Mixture-of-Experts models likely have lower depth than dense models. Overall, our results suggest that opaque serial depth is a useful tool for understanding the potential for models to do significant reasoning that is not externalized.
title_zh: 通过不透明的序列深度量化思维链的必要性
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）倾向于将其推理过程外化为思维链，这使得思维链成为监控的理想目标。这一现象部分源于Transformer架构的固有特性：当认知过程足够长时，必须通过思维链来实现（Korbak等，2025）。我们通过“不透明串行深度”这一概念对上述观点进行了形式化定义，该概念指在不使用可解释的中间步骤（如思维链）的情况下，能够完成的最长计算长度。基于这一形式化定义，我们计算了Gemma 3系列模型的不透明串行深度的数值上界，并推导出其他非标准LLM架构的渐近结果。此外，我们开源了一种自动化方法，可计算任意神经网络的不透明串行深度上界，并利用该方法证明：混合专家模型（Mixture-of-Experts）的深度很可能低于密集模型。总体而言，我们的研究结果表明，不透明串行深度是一个有助于理解模型进行重大但未外化的推理能力的有力工具。

title: Real-Time Trust Verification for Safe Agentic Actions using TrustBench
authors: Tavishi Sharma, Vinayak Sharma, Pragya Sharma
abstract: As large language models evolve from conversational assistants to autonomous agents, ensuring trustworthiness requires a fundamental shift from post-hoc evaluation to real-time action verification. Current frameworks like AgentBench evaluate task completion, while TrustLLM and HELM assess output quality after generation. However, none of these prevent harmful actions during agent execution. We present TrustBench, a dual-mode framework that (1) benchmarks trust across multiple dimensions using both traditional metrics and LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations, and (2) provides a toolkit agents invoke before taking actions to verify safety and reliability. Unlike existing approaches, TrustBench intervenes at the critical decision point: after an agent formulates an action but before execution. Domain-specific plugins encode specialized safety requirements for healthcare, finance, and technical domains. Across multiple agentic tasks, TrustBench reduced harmful actions by 87%. Domain-specific plugins outperformed generic verification, achieving 35% greater harm reduction. With sub-200ms latency, TrustBench enables practical real-time trust verification for autonomous agents.
title_zh: 基于TrustBench的实时信任验证以确保智能体安全行动
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型从对话助手演变为自主代理，确保其可信性需要从事后评估转向实时行为验证的根本性转变。当前的框架如AgentBench侧重于任务完成情况的评估，而TrustLLM和HELM则在生成后评估输出质量。然而，这些方法均无法在代理执行过程中防止有害行为的发生。我们提出TrustBench，一种双模式框架，（1）通过传统指标与“大模型作为裁判”（LLM-as-a-Judge）评估相结合，多维度地衡量信任度；（2）为代理提供一个工具包，在采取行动前进行安全性和可靠性验证。与现有方法不同，TrustBench在关键决策点介入：即代理生成动作之后、执行之前。针对医疗、金融和技术等特定领域设计的插件，嵌入了相应的专业安全要求。在多个代理任务中，TrustBench将有害行为减少了87%。相比通用验证方式，领域专用插件表现更优，有害行为减少幅度高出35%。TrustBench具备低于200毫秒的延迟，可实现自主代理实用化的实时可信性验证。

title: MiniAppBench: Evaluating the Shift from Text to Interactive HTML Responses in LLM-Powered Assistants
authors: Zuhao Zhang, Chengyue Yu, Yuante Li, Chenyi Zhuang, Linjian Mo, Shuai Li
abstract: With the rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) in code generation, human-AI interaction is evolving from static text responses to dynamic, interactive HTML-based applications, which we term MiniApps. These applications require models to not only render visual interfaces but also construct customized interaction logic that adheres to real-world principles. However, existing benchmarks primarily focus on algorithmic correctness or static layout reconstruction, failing to capture the capabilities required for this new paradigm. To address this gap, we introduce MiniAppBench, the first comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate principle-driven, interactive application generation. Sourced from a real-world application with 10M+ generations, MiniAppBench distills 500 tasks across six domains (e.g., Games, Science, and Tools). Furthermore, to tackle the challenge of evaluating open-ended interactions where no single ground truth exists, we propose MiniAppEval, an agentic evaluation framework. Leveraging browser automation, it performs human-like exploratory testing to systematically assess applications across three dimensions: Intention, Static, and Dynamic. Our experiments reveal that current LLMs still face significant challenges in generating high-quality MiniApps, while MiniAppEval demonstrates high alignment with human judgment, establishing a reliable standard for future research. Our code is available in github.com/MiniAppBench.
title_zh: MiniAppBench：评估大语言模型驱动助手中文本到交互式HTML响应的转变
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成领域的快速发展，人机交互正从静态文本响应演进为动态的、基于HTML的应用程序，我们将其称为MiniApps。这类应用不仅要求模型能够渲染可视化界面，还需构建符合现实世界原则的定制化交互逻辑。然而，现有的评估基准主要关注算法正确性或静态布局重建，未能涵盖这一新范式所必需的能力。为填补这一空白，我们提出了MiniAppBench——首个全面评估以原则为导向、具备交互能力的应用生成的基准。该基准源自一个真实应用，包含超过1000万次生成记录，从中提炼出涵盖六个领域（如游戏、科学和工具）的500个任务。此外，针对开放性交互评估中缺乏单一标准答案的挑战，我们提出了MiniAppEval——一种代理式评估框架。该框架利用浏览器自动化技术，模拟人类探索性测试，从三个维度系统评估应用程序：意图（Intention）、静态特性（Static）与动态行为（Dynamic）。实验结果表明，当前的LLMs在生成高质量MiniApps方面仍面临显著挑战；而MiniAppEval展现出与人类判断高度一致的评估效果，为未来研究建立了可靠的评价标准。相关代码已开源，可在github.com/MiniAppBench获取。

title: Evoking User Memory: Personalizing LLM via Recollection-Familiarity Adaptive Retrieval
authors: Yingyi Zhang, Junyi Li, Wenlin Zhang, Penyue Jia, Xianneng Li, Yichao Wang, Derong Xu, Yi Wen, Huifeng Guo, Yong Liu, Xiangyu Zhao
abstract: Personalized large language models (LLMs) rely on memory retrieval to incorporate user-specific histories, preferences, and contexts. Existing approaches either overload the LLM by feeding all the user's past memory into the prompt, which is costly and unscalable, or simplify retrieval into a one-shot similarity search, which captures only surface matches. Cognitive science, however, shows that human memory operates through a dual process: Familiarity, offering fast but coarse recognition, and Recollection, enabling deliberate, chain-like reconstruction for deeply recovering episodic content. Current systems lack both the ability to perform recollection retrieval and mechanisms to adaptively switch between the dual retrieval paths, leading to either insufficient recall or the inclusion of noise. To address this, we propose RF-Mem (Recollection-Familiarity Memory Retrieval), a familiarity uncertainty-guided dual-path memory retriever. RF-Mem measures the familiarity signal through the mean score and entropy. High familiarity leads to the direct top-K Familiarity retrieval path, while low familiarity activates the Recollection path. In the Recollection path, the system clusters candidate memories and applies alpha-mix with the query to iteratively expand evidence in embedding space, simulating deliberate contextual reconstruction. This design embeds human-like dual-process recognition into the retriever, avoiding full-context overhead and enabling scalable, adaptive personalization. Experiments across three benchmarks and corpus scales demonstrate that RF-Mem consistently outperforms both one-shot retrieval and full-context reasoning under fixed budget and latency constraints. Our code can be found in the Reproducibility Statement.
title_zh: 唤起用户记忆：通过回忆-熟悉度自适应检索实现大语言模型的个性化
abstract_zh:
个性化大语言模型（LLMs）依赖于记忆检索来融入用户特定的历史记录、偏好和上下文信息。现有方法要么将用户的全部历史记忆直接输入提示（prompt），导致大模型负担过重，成本高昂且难以扩展；要么将检索简化为单次相似性搜索，仅能捕捉表面匹配，缺乏深度理解。然而，认知科学表明，人类记忆运作遵循双过程机制：**熟悉性**（Familiarity）提供快速但粗略的识别，而**回忆**（Recollection）则支持有意识的、链式重构，以深入恢复情景性内容。当前系统既缺乏执行回忆式检索的能力，也缺少在双路径间自适应切换的机制，导致召回不足或引入噪声。

为解决上述问题，我们提出 **RF-Mem**（Recollection-Familiarity Memory Retrieval）——一种基于熟悉性不确定性引导的双路径记忆检索框架。RF-Mem通过均值得分与熵值来度量熟悉性信号：高熟悉性触发直接的Top-K熟悉性检索路径；低熟悉性则激活回忆路径。在回忆路径中，系统对候选记忆进行聚类，并利用查询与候选之间的α-混合（alpha-mix）在嵌入空间中迭代扩展证据，模拟有意识的情境重构过程。该设计将类人双过程识别机制融入检索器，避免了全上下文处理带来的开销，实现了可扩展且自适应的个性化。

在三个基准测试及不同规模语料库上的实验表明，RF-Mem 在固定预算和延迟约束下，始终优于单次检索与全上下文推理方法。我们的代码可在“可复现性声明”中获取。

title: Reinforced Generation of Combinatorial Structures: Ramsey Numbers
authors: Ansh Nagda, Prabhakar Raghavan, Abhradeep Thakurta
abstract: We present improved lower bounds for five classical Ramsey numbers: $\mathbf{R}(3, 13)$ is increased from $60$ to $61$, $\mathbf{R}(3, 18)$ from $99$ to $100$, $\mathbf{R}(4, 13)$ from $138$ to $139$, $\mathbf{R}(4, 14)$ from $147$ to $148$, and $\mathbf{R}(4, 15)$ from $158$ to $159$. These results were achieved using~\emph{AlphaEvolve}, an LLM-based code mutation agent. Beyond these new results, we successfully recovered lower bounds for all Ramsey numbers known to be exact, and matched the best known lower bounds across many other cases. These include bounds for which previous work does not detail the algorithms used. Virtually all known Ramsey lower bounds are derived computationally, with bespoke search algorithms each delivering a handful of results. AlphaEvolve is a single meta-algorithm yielding search algorithms for all of our results.
title_zh: 组合结构的强化生成：拉姆齐数
abstract_zh: 我们提出了五个经典拉姆齐数的改进下界：$\mathbf{R}(3, 13)$ 从 60 提升至 61，$\mathbf{R}(3, 18)$ 从 99 提升至 100，$\mathbf{R}(4, 13)$ 从 138 提升至 139，$\mathbf{R}(4, 14)$ 从 147 提升至 148，$\mathbf{R}(4, 15)$ 从 158 提升至 159。这些成果是通过基于大语言模型（LLM）的代码变异代理——\emph{AlphaEvolve} 实现的。除了上述新结果外，我们还成功复现了所有已知精确的拉姆齐数下界，并在众多其他情形中达到了当前最佳已知下界。其中一些结果此前的文献并未详细说明所用算法。事实上，几乎所有已知的拉姆齐数下界都是通过计算获得的，且每项结果通常依赖于特定的搜索算法，仅能产出少量成果。而 AlphaEvolve 是一个统一的元算法，能够自动生成适用于所有上述结果的搜索算法。

title: AutoAgent: Evolving Cognition and Elastic Memory Orchestration for Adaptive Agents
authors: Xiaoxing Wang, Ning Liao, Shikun Wei, Chen Tang, Feiyu Xiong
abstract: Autonomous agent frameworks still struggle to reconcile long-term experiential learning with real-time, context-sensitive decision-making. In practice, this gap appears as static cognition, rigid workflow dependence, and inefficient context usage, which jointly limit adaptability in open-ended and non-stationary environments. To address these limitations, we present AutoAgent, a self-evolving multi-agent framework built on three tightly coupled components: evolving cognition, on-the-fly contextual decision-making, and elastic memory orchestration. At the core of AutoAgent, each agent maintains structured prompt-level cognition over tools, self-capabilities, peer expertise, and task knowledge. During execution, this cognition is combined with live task context to select actions from a unified space that includes tool calls, LLM-based generation, and inter-agent requests. To support efficient long-horizon reasoning, an Elastic Memory Orchestrator dynamically organizes interaction history by preserving raw records, compressing redundant trajectories, and constructing reusable episodic abstractions, thereby reducing token overhead while retaining decision-critical evidence. These components are integrated through a closed-loop cognitive evolution process that aligns intended actions with observed outcomes to continuously update cognition and expand reusable skills, without external retraining. Empirical results across retrieval-augmented reasoning, tool-augmented agent benchmarks, and embodied task environments show that AutoAgent consistently improves task success, tool-use efficiency, and collaborative robustness over static and memory-augmented baselines. Overall, AutoAgent provides a unified and practical foundation for adaptive autonomous agents that must learn from experience while making reliable context-aware decisions in dynamic environments.
title_zh: AutoAgent：面向自适应智能体的演化认知与弹性记忆编排
abstract_zh: 自主代理框架在实现长期经验学习与实时、上下文敏感决策的协调方面仍面临挑战。实践中，这一差距表现为静态认知、流程僵化依赖以及低效的上下文利用，共同限制了其在开放性、非平稳环境中的适应能力。为解决这些局限，我们提出AutoAgent——一种基于三个紧密耦合组件的自演化多代理框架：持续演化的认知、动态上下文驱动的决策机制，以及弹性记忆编排。在AutoAgent的核心架构中，每个代理都维护着关于工具、自我能力、同伴专长及任务知识的结构化提示级认知。执行过程中，该认知与实时任务上下文相结合，从统一的动作空间中选择行动，该空间涵盖工具调用、基于大语言模型的生成以及代理间请求。为支持高效的长周期推理，弹性记忆编排器通过保留原始交互记录、压缩冗余轨迹，并构建可复用的情景抽象，动态组织交互历史，在降低token开销的同时保留关键决策证据。这些组件通过一个闭环认知演化过程实现整合，使预期行为与实际结果对齐，从而持续更新认知并扩展可复用技能，无需外部重新训练。在检索增强推理、工具增强型代理基准测试以及具身任务环境中的实证结果表明，相较于静态和记忆增强基线，AutoAgent在任务成功率、工具使用效率以及协作鲁棒性方面均表现出持续提升。总体而言，AutoAgent为必须从经验中学习并能在动态环境中做出可靠上下文感知决策的自适应自主代理提供了一个统一且实用的基础。

title: An Empirical Study of Interaction Smells in Multi-Turn Human-LLM Collaborative Code Generation
authors: Binquan Zhang, Li Zhang, Lin Shi, Song Wang, Yuwei Qian, Linhui Zhao, Fang Liu, An Fu, Yida Ye
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized code generation, evolving from static tools into dynamic conversational interfaces that facilitate complex, multi-turn collaborative programming. While LLMs exhibit remarkable proficiency in generating standalone code snippets, they often struggle to maintain contextual consistency during extended interactions, creating significant obstacles in the collaboration process. Existing benchmarks primarily emphasize the functional correctness of the final output, overlooking latent quality issues within the interaction process itself, which we term Interaction Smells. In this paper, we conduct an empirical study on sampled real-word user-LLM interactions from WildChat and LMSYS-Chat-1M datasets to systematically investigate Interaction Smells in human-LLM code generation tasks from the perspectives of phenomena, distribution, and mitigation. First, we establish the first taxonomy of Interaction Smells by manually performing open card sorting on real-world interaction logs. This taxonomy categorizes Interaction Smells into three primary categories, i.e., User Intent Quality, Historical Instruction Compliance, and Historical Response Violation, comprising nine specific subcategories. Next, we quantitatively evaluate six mainstream LLMs (i.e., GPT-4o, DeepSeek-Chat, Gemini 2.5, Qwen2.5-32B, Qwen2.5-72B, and Qwen3-235B-a22b) to analyze the distribution of Interaction Smells across different models. Finally, we propose Invariant-aware Constraint Evolution (InCE), a multi-agent framework designed to improve multi-turn interaction quality through explicit extraction of global invariants and pre-generation quality audits. Experimental results on the extended WildBench benchmark demonstrate that this lightweight mitigation approach significantly improves the Task Success Rate and effectively suppresses the occurrence of Interaction Smells.
title_zh: 多轮人机协同代码生成中交互异味的实证研究
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）已彻底改变了代码生成领域，从静态工具演变为动态的对话式接口，支持复杂、多轮次的人机协同编程。尽管LLMs在生成独立代码片段方面表现出卓越能力，但在长时间交互过程中往往难以保持上下文一致性，从而给协作流程带来显著障碍。现有的评估基准主要关注最终输出的功能正确性，却忽视了交互过程本身潜藏的质量问题，我们将其称为“交互异味”（Interaction Smells）。本文通过对WildChat和LMSYS-Chat-1M数据集中采样的真实用户-LLM交互记录进行实证研究，系统地从现象、分布及缓解三个角度探讨人机代码生成任务中的交互异味。

首先，我们通过人工对真实交互日志进行开放式卡片分类，建立了首个交互异味的分类体系。该分类体系将交互异味划分为三大类：用户意图质量、历史指令遵循度以及历史响应违背性，共包含九个具体子类别。其次，我们定量评估了六种主流LLM（即GPT-4o、DeepSeek-Chat、Gemini 2.5、Qwen2.5-32B、Qwen2.5-72B 和 Qwen3-235B-a22b），分析不同模型在交互异味分布上的差异。最后，我们提出一种名为“不变量感知的约束演化”（Invariant-aware Constraint Evolution, InCE）的多智能体框架，通过显式提取全局不变量并进行生成前质量审计，以提升多轮交互的整体质量。

在扩展版WildBench基准上的实验结果表明，这一轻量级缓解方法显著提升了任务成功率，并有效抑制了交互异味的发生。

title: Class Model Generation from Requirements using Large Language Models
authors: Jackson Nguyen, Rui En Koe, Fanyu Wang, Chetan Arora, Alessio Ferrari
abstract: The emergence of Large Language Models (LLMs) has opened new opportunities to automate software engineering activities that traditionally require substantial manual effort. Among these, class diagram generation represents a critical yet resource-intensive phase in software design. This paper investigates the capabilities of state-of-the-art LLMs, including GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4.0, Gemini 2.5 Flash Thinking, and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, to generate UML class diagrams from natural language requirements automatically. To evaluate the effectiveness and reliability of LLM-based model generation, we propose a comprehensive dual-validation framework that integrates an LLM-as-a-Judge methodology with human-in-the-loop assessment. Using eight heterogeneous datasets, we apply chain-of-thought prompting to extract domain entities, attributes, and associations, generating corresponding PlantUML representations. The resulting models are evaluated across five quality dimensions: completeness, correctness, conformance to standards, comprehensibility, and terminological alignment. Two independent LLM judges (Grok and Mistral) perform structured pairwise comparisons, and their judgments are further validated against expert evaluations. Our results demonstrate that LLMs can generate structurally coherent and semantically meaningful UML diagrams, achieving substantial alignment with human evaluators. The consistency observed between LLM-based and human-based assessments highlights the potential of LLMs not only as modeling assistants but also as reliable evaluators in automated requirements engineering workflows, offering practical insights into the capabilities and limitations of LLM-driven UML class diagram automation.
title_zh: 使用大型语言模型从需求生成类模型
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的出现为自动化传统上需要大量人工投入的软件工程活动开辟了新的机遇。在这些活动中，类图生成是软件设计中一个关键但资源密集的阶段。本文研究了当前最先进的LLMs——包括GPT-5、Claude Sonnet 4.0、Gemini 2.5 Flash Thinking以及Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct——从自然语言需求自动生成UML类图的能力。为了评估基于LLM的建模生成在有效性与可靠性方面的表现，我们提出了一套综合性的双重验证框架，该框架结合了“LLM作为裁判”方法与人工参与的评估机制。利用八个异构数据集，我们采用思维链提示（chain-of-thought prompting）技术提取领域实体、属性及关联关系，并生成相应的PlantUML表示。所生成的模型在五个质量维度上进行评估：完整性、正确性、标准符合性、可读性以及术语一致性。两名独立的LLM裁判（Grok和Mistral）执行结构化的成对比较，其判断结果进一步与专家评估结果进行比对验证。实验结果表明，LLMs能够生成结构合理且语义清晰的UML类图，与人类评估者具有高度的一致性。LLM评估与人工评估之间表现出的高度一致性，凸显了LLMs不仅可作为建模辅助工具，更具备在自动化需求工程流程中担任可靠评估者的潜力，为理解LLM驱动的UML类图自动生成技术的能力与局限提供了切实可行的洞见。
